commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: (However there came other boats from Tiberias near unto the place where they did eat bread, after the Lord had given thanks:) Cornelius a Lapide But there came, &c. We can see from this verse that the place where Christ multiplied the loaves was near Tiberias, and therefore that those who sailed from thence to Bethsaida and Capharnaum must have sailed past Tiberias. The meaning Isaiah , the report of the miracle being spread abroad, many both from other places as well as Tiberias, came to the place where the miracle was wrought, that they might see and hear Jesus who had done such great things.
